metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command and test-file location for AC4 (e.g., add a test case in `tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py` or a dedicated integration fixture, and supply the pytest invocation as the proving command).
findings.finding: AC4 ('a test asserts the detector's finding surfaces through the code-review advisory path as an advisory, never blocking') has no proving command and no test file location, leaving it unverifiable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — fourth bullet (AC4)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: 'Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py::test_finding_is_advisory -v`' and add a test case that instantiates the detector, calls it, and asserts the returned finding has `blocking=False` (or the equivalent advisory flag in the codebase's finding schema).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete JSON field and value that represents 'down-ranked' in `criteria_routing.json` (e.g., `"priority": "low"` or `"suppress": true`), so the unit test assertion and the implementation target the same schema.
findings.finding: 'Down-ranked' is used throughout the routing AC and body but the plan never states the concrete JSON field/value that constitutes 'down-ranked', leaving the executor to invent the representation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet (AC1); 'What' section bullet 1; 'Detector mechanism' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line example to AC1, e.g.: 'The routing entry for `docs` and `llm-prompts` must have `"blocking": false` and `"priority": "suppressed"` (or equivalent lowest-rank value used by other entries in the file).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or supplement the claimed use of `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` for ADDED-DEFINITION detection: the existing function extracts only `import` references from added lines, not `def`/`class`/exported symbol definitions — specify the actual mechanism (e.g., a diff-scoped ctags sub-index of added lines, or a new regex pattern matching `def`/`class`/`async def` in `+` lines) that actually identifies newly-added exported symbols.
findings.finding: `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` does NOT extract newly-added exported/public function/method/class definitions — it exclusively extracts import references (kind=import) from added lines. The plan's detector mechanism rests on this function finding 'newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS', a capability the function does not have.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET: parse the unified diff into (file, added-lines); use the existing diff/reference machinery (`rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) build a new helper that filters diff `+`-lines for `def`, `async def`, `class` patterns and uses ctags on a temp file of those lines to identify language-aware definitions, or (b) add a dedicated regex pass over the `+` lines matching Python/JS/etc. definition forms. Do not reuse `extract_references_from_diff` for this purpose — it is an import-extractor, not a definition-extractor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the actual ast-grep or ctags integration path for identifying newly-added exported symbol definitions: the Engine B ast-grep lane runs pre-authored YAML rules, not a runtime query API — either author a YAML ast-grep rule for definition detection or describe using a ctags sub-index on the diff's added lines instead.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the detector uses 'the ast-grep fast lane' to identify newly-added exported symbol definitions, but the Engine B ast-grep integration runs pre-authored YAML structural rules (not a runtime diff-query API), and there is no ast-grep-backed API for extracting symbol definitions from a diff at query time.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'use the existing diff/reference machinery (`rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS in the added lines (language-aware via the grounding backend)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the concrete invocation mechanism for the Python `new_behavior_without_test` detector: `run_detectors` currently only invokes Engine B YAML-rule detectors (not Python callables) — specify whether a new Python-callable registration path must be added to `run_detectors`, or whether the detector is invoked via a separate workflow step outside the DET slice.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new `new_behavior_without_test` Python detector is 'registered as a code-review detector' and 'invoked in the code-review DET phase (the `run_detectors` slice)', but `run_detectors` as implemented only invokes Engine B YAML-rule detectors registered in `criteria_routing.json` with `exec: "DET"` — a Python callable in `detectors.py` is not automatically picked up by this mechanism. The integration path is unspecified and unverified.
findings.location: Detector mechanism / ARCHITECTURE NOTE section: 'It is registered as a code-review detector, not authored as a builtin YAML smell rule' and 'invoked in the code-review DET phase (the `run_detectors` slice)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the ADDED-DEFINITION SET mechanism: `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` extracts import names, not exported symbol definitions — specify the correct grounding path (e.g. `resolve.build_index` on a virtual file of added lines, or an ast-grep structural pattern matching `def`/`class` on `+`-prefixed diff lines) for identifying newly-added public definitions.
findings.finding: resolve.extract_references_from_diff extracts IMPORT references (Python `import`/`from … import …` statements) from added diff lines — not exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS. The plan claims to use this function to 'identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS in the added lines (language-aware via the grounding backend)', which is factually incorrect about what the function does.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the extract_references_from_diff reference in the detector mechanism with: parse `+`-prefixed diff lines to a temporary file fragment, then call `resolve.build_index` (ctags) or an ast-grep pattern to identify added `def`/`class` declarations — or use the polyglot `_REMOVED_DECL_RE`-style regex (already in registry.py) adapted to ADDED lines as the heuristic fallback.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete schema field/mechanism that implements 'down-ranked' in criteria_routing.json (e.g. add a `"surface_priority": "low"` field, or `"suppressed": true`) so the AC unit test has a named property to assert on.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'down-rank/suppress' docs and llm-prompts in criteria_routing.json, but the routing schema has no 'priority', 'rank', or 'suppressed' field today. The AC says 'unit test asserts the packaged routing marks both criteria non-blocking AND down-ranked vs the other advisory criteria' — but there is no field name or mechanism in the schema to encode 'down-ranked'. The implementer has no concrete schema target to write or test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria bullet 1 / What section (down-rank/suppress)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and name the concrete file/mechanism for suppressing the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern', or remove it from the What section if it is handled implicitly by the docs/llm-prompts down-ranking already specified.
findings.finding: The plan names 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern' as a suppression target in the What section, but no such pattern, criterion, configuration key, or code location for it exists anywhere in the codebase. No file is named as the edit target for this suppression.
findings.location: What section: 'Down-rank/suppress… the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether criteria_routing.json needs a new `new-behavior-without-test` entry (exec=DET or exec=AGENT with advisory posture) for the detector to be dispatched by run_detectors and for threshold_for to produce the specified advisory/non-blocking result.
findings.finding: The new `new_behavior_without_test` detector in detectors.py will produce findings under a new criterion, but criteria_routing.json (the routing index that drives threshold_for, blocking_enabled, and DET criterion registration) is not listed as needing a new entry for this criterion. Without a routing entry, the criterion will be unknown to threshold_for (defaulting to 0.95 advisory — acceptable) but also absent from det_criteria() if it's a DET criterion, and the AC test 'asserts the detector's finding surfaces through the code-review advisory path as an advisory' may rely on routing behavior the plan does not configure.
findings.location: Scope/files section; AC bullet 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the misattributed mechanism: specify how the detector actually finds newly-added public definitions in a diff — either (a) a regex/AST scan of the diff's `+` lines for bare `def`/`class`/`fn` signatures (fast, polyglot, honest about being heuristic) or (b) a ctags `build_index` call on HEAD vs BASE to diff the symbol tables; remove the claim that `extract_references_from_diff` or the ctags refutation lane serves this role.
findings.finding: The plan's core mechanism for finding 'newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS' is misattributed to `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep 'symbol lanes'. In the actual codebase, `extract_references_from_diff` (resolve.py:707-717) extracts only Python IMPORT references (kind=import) from added diff lines — it parses `import`/`from X import` statements, not function/class definition signatures. The ctags Engine A lane (`refute_absence`, `build_index`) is designed to look up whether a named symbol EXISTS in the full repo index, not to identify which symbols are newly DEFINED in a diff. Neither tool surfaces 'added exported def/class definitions'. The plan's stated mechanism won't achieve its goal as described.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET: parse the unified diff into (file, added-lines); use the existing diff/reference machinery (`rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the ADDED-DEFINITION SET description with: 'Parse the unified diff's `+` lines for public definition signatures using the same polyglot regex patterns already in `registry._REMOVED_DECL_PATTERNS` (which covers Python `def`, `class`, Go `func`, Rust `fn`, JS `function`/arrow) but filtered to non-underscore names (heuristic for public/exported). This is an honest heuristic, consistent with the existing `content_triggered_overlays` approach, and avoids misusing the import-extraction or refutation lanes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `new-behavior-without-test` entry to the `criteria_routing.json` edit scope specifying `exec`, `detector` selector (id or id_prefix), `blocking_enabled: false`, `fail_mode: open`, and `default_posture: advisory` — mirroring the conflict-markers pattern for an advisory DET criterion.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify a `criteria_routing.json` entry for the new `new-behavior-without-test` criterion. The existing DET infrastructure (registry.py `det_criteria()`, `run_detectors`) discovers runnable DET criteria exclusively by reading `exec: DET` entries from `routing_index()`. Without a routing entry, the new detector is never discovered or invoked by the DET machinery. The plan's file scope section lists `criteria_routing.json` only for docs/llm-prompts down-ranking.
findings.location: Scope / files section — 'src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing)' — and AC bullet 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify or redesign the surfacing path: either (a) specify that the new detector injects a synthetic finding into the merge_findings input (so it flows through Pass-2/Pass-3 like LLM findings) and describe how; or (b) acknowledge it surfaces in coverage only (like conflict-markers) and update AC4 accordingly; or (c) specify a new bridge in `apply_failclosed`/`code_review_coach` that promotes advisory DET matches to the `surfaced` list.
findings.finding: The plan states the detector's finding 'surfaces through the existing `code_review_coach` path' as advisory, but the existing DET infrastructure routes detector outcomes to `verdict['coverage']['security_detectors']` (a coverage bucket) or forces a BLOCK — not to the `surfaced` advisory findings list that `code_review_coach_inputs` reads. LLM findings enter `code_review_coach` via merge_findings → Pass-2 → Pass-3 decide → `surfaced`. DET findings go through `apply_failclosed` which populates `verdict['blocking']` (for blocking matches) or `verdict['coverage']` (for advisory/abstain). There is no existing path from an advisory DET match into the `surfaced` list that feeds the coach.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'Coaching surfaces through the existing `code_review_coach` path'; AC bullet 4 — 'A test asserts the detector's finding, when it fires, surfaces through the code-review advisory path as an advisory (never blocking) at this rollout'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' mechanism: `extract_references_from_diff` extracts *import* references, not function/class definitions — specify the actual mechanism for finding newly-added exported symbol definitions (e.g., regex over `+def`/`+class` lines in the diff, then filtered by public-name convention, or a ctags before/after diff), distinct from the import-reference extractor.
findings.finding: `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` extracts Python *import* references only — not exported symbol DEFINITIONS. The plan's claim that this function is used to 'identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS' is an unverified capability assertion that is falsified by the actual implementation.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET: parse the unified diff into (file, added-lines); use the existing diff/reference machinery (`rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `extract_references_from_diff` citation in the ADDED-DEFINITION SET step with an explicit diff-line parser that matches `+def <name>` / `+class <name>` patterns on added lines (filtering out `_`-prefixed names for privacy), then uses the ctags index to confirm the symbol is unique/new. The TEST-CORRELATION step can then look for that name in added test-file lines — which is a straightforward regex, not requiring the grounding oracle at all.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the ctags index alone cannot determine which definitions are 'newly added' vs pre-existing — specify whether the detector uses (a) direct regex on `+def`/`+class` diff lines (no ctags needed for this step) or (b) a before/after ctags snapshot diff, and update the mechanism description accordingly.
findings.finding: `resolve.build_index` (the ctags 'symbol lane') indexes the full repo on disk and has no mechanism to distinguish which definitions are *newly added* (in `+` lines of the diff) versus pre-existing. Using it as the 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' source is an unverified capability assertion.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes) to identify newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS in the added lines (language-aware via the grounding backend)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct or replace the plan's reference to `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` for the ADDED-DEFINITION SET step — this function extracts import references (kind=import), not exported symbol definitions (`def`/`class`); specify the actual mechanism (e.g., a new regex over added diff lines matching `def`/`class` patterns as in `content_triggered_overlays`, or an ast-grep/ctags scan of the changed files).
findings.finding: `extract_references_from_diff` extracts import-references from added lines (kind=import, Python-only), not exported symbol DEFINITIONS — the plan's 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' step relies on it for finding 'newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS' but the actual function does no such thing.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / AC line 2 ('using the real ast-grep/ctags grounding machinery via resolve.extract_references_from_diff + symbol lanes to find added exported symbols')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' mechanism description with: parse added `+` lines of the unified diff for new `def`/`class` signatures (using a polyglot pattern set analogous to `_REMOVED_DECL_PATTERNS` in registry.py but on added lines), and resolve their public/exported status via ctags `build_index` on the post-merge repo state. The existing `extract_references_from_diff` should be removed from this description as it serves a different purpose.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the integration point for the `new_behavior_without_test` Python detector: either (a) add a separate invocation in `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` alongside `apply_failclosed`, injecting findings directly into the verdict's advisory list; or (b) define a new 'Python-callable DET' extension point in `run_detectors`; and specify which criterion string the finding carries so `threshold_for` resolves it as non-blocking.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the `new_behavior_without_test` detector as a Python callable in `code_review/detectors.py` 'invoked in the code-review DET phase (the `run_detectors` slice)', but `run_detectors` exclusively operates via `engine_b.scan` over YAML-rule-backed detectors from the grounding registry — there is no integration point for a Python callable, and no new routing schema field or invocation hook is specified.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section: 'ARCHITECTURE NOTE (honest): this is a PYTHON detector in code_review/detectors.py, invoked in the code-review DET phase (the run_detectors slice)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete schema field and value vocabulary that encodes 'down-ranked / suppressed' in `criteria_routing.json` (e.g. `surface_priority: "low"` vs `"normal"`, or `suppress_from_surface: true`), specify where the surfacing logic reads it, and restate the AC unit test assertion in terms of that field.
findings.finding: The plan promises to 'down-rank / suppress' `docs` and `llm-prompts` from 'the surfaced advisory set' via `criteria_routing.json`, but the existing routing schema has no `priority`, `rank`, `suppress`, or surface-gating field — only `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`, `default_posture`, `exec`, and `applies_to`. The mechanism that would implement 'down-ranked vs the other advisory criteria' is undefined.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'A unit test asserts the packaged routing marks both criteria non-blocking AND down-ranked vs the other advisory criteria.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `surface_priority` field (values: `"normal"` | `"low"`) to the routing schema; set `docs` and `llm-prompts` to `"low"`; have the surfacing/coach step skip or defer `"low"` criteria findings; assert `routing['docs']['surface_priority'] == 'low'` in the unit test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify whether `new-behavior-without-test` is added to `OVERLAY_IDS` and to `criteria_routing.json` (with what `exec`, `blocking_enabled`, and `block_threshold` fields), or document that the detector bypasses the overlay enum entirely and explain how its findings reach the coach path without an OVERLAY_IDS entry.
findings.finding: The `new-behavior-without-test` criterion string is not added to `OVERLAY_IDS` in `registry.py` (the closed enum that governs criterion vocabulary throughout the workflow), yet the plan says findings 'surface through the existing code_review_coach path as an advisory' — the path that depends on criteria flowing through Pass-3 decide with a known threshold, and coaching triggered by criterion membership.
findings.location: AC line 4: 'A test asserts the detector's finding, when it fires, surfaces through the code-review advisory path as an advisory (never blocking) at this rollout.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `criteria_routing.json` entry for `new-behavior-without-test` with `exec: "DET"` (or a new exec type), `blocking_enabled: false`, `block_threshold: 0.95`, `fail_mode: "open"` — this makes threshold_for resolve it deterministically and documents the advisory-only posture explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `extract_references_from_diff` extracts import-statement names (not definition sites), and specify what mechanism actually detects newly-added public `def`/`class` definitions in added diff lines (e.g. a new regex analogous to `_REMOVED_DECL_RE` in `registry.py`, or ctags restricted to added-line offsets).
findings.finding: `extract_references_from_diff` extracts `kind=import` references (Python import-statement names) — NOT definition sites — so the plan's claim that it reuses this function to find 'newly-ADDED exported/public function/method/class DEFINITIONS' is semantically incorrect. The actual ADDED-DEFINITION work is unspecified new logic, not a reuse.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section; AC line 'use the real ast-grep/ctags grounding machinery (via resolve.extract_references_from_diff + symbol lanes) to find added exported symbols'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the `extract_references_from_diff` citation with a description of a new diff-parse step (regex over `+` lines for `def`/`class` signatures), pointing to the existing `_REMOVED_DECL_RE` pattern in `registry.py` as the model to adapt for added lines.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what routing key in `criteria_routing.json` achieves 'de-prioritise / suppress' for docs and llm-prompts (name the field, and identify which consumer/code path reads and acts on it), or confirm this is achieved by an existing mechanism (e.g. `block_threshold` already at 0.95 is the only ranking lever).
findings.finding: The plan says `criteria_routing.json` will 'de-prioritise / suppress' docs and llm-prompts surfaces but does not specify what routing key achieves this. No such field (e.g. `priority_weight`, `surface_rank`, `suppressed`) exists in the current routing schema or in the `registry.py` consumers — adding one would proliferate config surface.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'kept non-blocking AND de-prioritised / suppressed from the surfaced advisory set'; What section: 'Down-rank/suppress the docs and llm-prompts advisory surfaces'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' mechanism: `extract_references_from_diff` extracts import references (not definition sites) and the ctags symbol lanes refute named symbols (not enumerate new definitions) — either spec the new diff-scoped definition-extractor that must be built, or reframe the detector mechanism as new regex/heuristic logic that does not claim to reuse those named surfaces.
findings.finding: `extract_references_from_diff` and the ctags/ast-grep symbol lanes do not provide the 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' capability the plan asserts — they extract import references and refute named symbols respectively, and cannot enumerate newly-added exported function/method/class definitions from a diff.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'ADDED-DEFINITION SET' step; `src/rebar/grounding/resolve.py:707-717` (extract_references_from_diff); `src/rebar/grounding/resolve.py:424-487` (refute_absence / build_index)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the ADDED-DEFINITION SET description with an honest account of what must be built: a new function (e.g., `extract_definitions_from_diff`) that parses `+` diff lines with a language-aware regex for `def`/`class`/`function` declarations marked as exported/public (analogous to `_REMOVED_DECL_RE` already in `registry.py` for the `deletion-impact` overlay), then uses `build_index` only for the TEST-CORRELATION step (looking up whether any added symbol name appears in a test file's ctags index). This makes the reuse honest — ctags is used for test-file correlation, not definition extraction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC1) that explicitly verifies the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern' is suppressed/reclassified in `criteria_routing.json`, including a test assertion for it.
findings.finding: The 'What' section describes a third deliverable — fixing the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern' — but no acceptance criterion verifies this fix was made or tests it.
findings.location: ## What (third bullet in the suppression description) vs AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1's unit test to also assert that the routing entry for the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling' pattern is marked non-blocking and down-ranked (or suppressed), mirroring the existing assertion structure for docs + llm-prompts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line (and a corresponding test assertion in `test_code_review_nit_routing.py`) that verifies the 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern' is suppressed/down-ranked in the updated routing config.
findings.finding: The 'documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling false-major pattern' suppression is named in the What section but has no corresponding AC line or test assertion, leaving it without an end-state-observable proof.
findings.location: ## What section (third bullet) vs. ## Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC-1 with a clause such as: 'A unit test also asserts that the documentation-ahead-of-a-landed-sibling pattern is marked suppressed/non-major in the routing config.' Add a corresponding parametrized test case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a regression test case (e) to `tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py` asserting that the detector ABSTAINS (returns no finding, does not raise) when the grounding backend is unavailable/un-initialised.
findings.finding: The plan guarantees the detector ABSTAINS when no grounding backend is available, but the regression test suite (AC bullet 3) lists only four cases — none of which tests 'no backend available.' If the detector raises an exception instead of returning no finding in that scenario, the advisory/permissive contract is silently violated in production.
findings.location: AC bullet 2 (ABSTAIN conditions) vs AC bullet 3 (test cases a–d)
findings.suggested_fix: In `test_new_behavior_detector.py`, add a test that patches/mocks the grounding resolve layer to raise `BackendUnavailableError` (or equivalent) and asserts `detect()` returns an empty finding list without propagating the exception.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit timeout (e.g., `GROUNDING_TIMEOUT_S = 10`) on all grounding backend calls inside the `new_behavior_without_test` detector, and add a corresponding ABSTAIN branch for the timeout/error case, so the DET phase is never blocked by a hung external process.
findings.finding: The detector invokes grounding backends (ast-grep, ctags — external process calls) with no stated timeout or hang-protection. The ABSTAIN conditions listed cover 'no grounding backend available' and 'unparseable diff' but NOT a slow or hung backend process. A hanging grounding call would block the DET phase synchronously with no escape hatch.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / AC line 2: 'ABSTAINS (returns no finding, never raises) on an unparseable/ambiguous diff, a docs/config-only diff, or when no grounding backend is available'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap grounding calls in a `try/except` with a `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` (or equivalent async timeout) guard and treat any timeout or unexpected exception as an ABSTAIN — log a WARNING with the exception type so operators can see it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one structured log line (e.g., `logger.debug/info`) for each major branch (fired, abstained-reason, error) in the `new_behavior_without_test` detector, and call out this instrumentation requirement in the ACs.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the new detector: when it fires, abstains, or encounters an unexpected error, there are no logs, metrics, or traces called out. Operators have no visibility into whether the detector is functioning, silently abstaining on every review, or hitting backend errors.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / AC line 2 / Scope / files section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a runtime disable mechanism (e.g., an environment variable `REBAR_DETECTOR_NEW_BEHAVIOR_ENABLED=false` or a config field in `criteria_routing.json`) so the detector can be turned off instantly without a redeploy, and document this as the rollback procedure.
findings.finding: Neither change has a runtime feature flag or config toggle: 'rollback' is described as a code-hunk revert, which requires a full redeploy cycle. If the detector causes DET-phase hangs or unexpected behavior in production, there is no cheap, instant kill-switch short of a new deployment.
findings.location: Scope cohesion section: 'INDEPENDENTLY revertible (the routing edit and the detector are separate hunks)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single `if not settings.get('new_behavior_without_test_enabled', True): return []` guard at the top of the detector, controlled by an env-var or the existing routing config.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new ADDED-definition scanner should reuse or extend the existing `_REMOVED_DECL_RE` / `content_triggered_overlays` machinery in `registry.py` (same diff-line scan, just on `+` lines) rather than re-implementing polyglot def-detection in `detectors.py`.
findings.finding: The plan proposes new ADDED-definition detection logic in `code_review/detectors.py`, but `registry.py` already contains `_REMOVED_DECL_RE` — a polyglot regex over diff lines for `def/class/function/fn/func` signatures — for the `content_triggered_overlays` / `deletion-impact` path. The new detector needs the mirror-image of that logic (for `+` lines instead of `-` lines) and should extend or reuse it rather than reinventing it in a separate file.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section; 'ARCHITECTURE NOTE: this is a PYTHON detector in code_review/detectors.py'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'e.g.' with 'the authoritative test-glob list is:' and enumerate the exact globs the detector will use, so the test fixtures and the implementation share the same matching logic.
findings.finding: The test glob list for test-correlation is introduced with 'e.g.', leaving the actual authoritative set of test globs to executor discretion and making the MUST-NOT-FIRE and ABSTAIN test cases ambiguous.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'TEST-CORRELATION' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the Python code-review detector pattern (vs. YAML builtin smell rule) in a persistent artifact — e.g. add a note to CLAUDE.md or `docs/grounding.md` — so future contributors know when to use each tier.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — a Python-coded detector registered in the code-review DET phase, distinct from the YAML smell-rule pattern — but does not commit to capturing this in any persistent artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc). The 'ARCHITECTURE NOTE' exists only in this ticket, not in the repo.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section / 'ARCHITECTURE NOTE (honest)' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Code-review detectors' section to `docs/grounding.md` (or CLAUDE.md) explaining: (1) simple single-pattern checks → YAML builtin smell rule; (2) multi-step diff-parse + cross-file correlation checks → Python detector in `code_review/detectors.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the test-file glob patterns configurable (e.g., read from `criteria_routing.json`, a `pyproject.toml` `[tool.rebar]` table, or a dedicated config key) rather than hardcoding them in the detector, so they can evolve without a code change.
findings.finding: The test-file glob patterns used by the detector (`tests/`, `test_*.py`, `*_test.*`) are hardcoded literals in the plan and, by implication, in the implementation. These heuristics are likely to evolve as the repo grows (e.g., new test directories, JS test files, pytest-discovery config changes), but no configuration point is mentioned.
findings.location: Detector mechanism section — 'TEST-CORRELATION' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `test_globs` key to `criteria_routing.json` (or a dedicated `code_review/detectors_config.json`) with a default list; have the detector read from there, falling back to the current defaults.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief 'callers / downstream consumers' note to the plan (or a rollout section) that enumerates who currently consumes docs/llm-prompts advisories and confirms they are tolerant of suppression (or will be notified).
findings.finding: The plan changes existing routing behavior for `docs` and `llm-prompts` advisories but does not acknowledge downstream consumers or workflows that currently depend on receiving those advisory findings, leaving potential breakage unaddressed.
findings.location: ## What section; ## Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art note documenting why a textual heuristic in the advisory path is preferred over (or complementary to) existing diff-coverage tools (e.g., `diff-cover`, `coverage.py` new-line mode, Codecov PR delta) — even one sentence suffices.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel Python detector for 'new behavior without test' without checking whether established tools (SonarQube 'new code' coverage, Codecov PR coverage diff, coverage.py `--fail-under` on new lines, or GitHub's own 'changed files' + test coverage integration) already address this at the CI/CD layer — no prior-art consideration is documented.
findings.location: Why / What sections: 'the highest-value landmine - new behavior shipped with no test - is buried by the old scalar (0.415). We want less nit friction and a reliable catch for the landmine class.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Why' or 'Detector mechanism' section: 'Runtime coverage tools (diff-cover, coverage.py --fail-under on new lines) require a test run and a coverage report in CI; this detector fires at review time, before CI, on a textual signal — the two are complementary, not duplicates.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
